% Sample Schuller lecture fragment for testing
% Tests macro expansion, IEEEeqnarray, theorem environments

\bd
Let $(M, \cO)$ be a topological space and let $p \in M$.
A \emph{neighbourhood} of $p$ is a set $N \se M$ such that
\bse
\exists U \in \cO \cl p \in U \se N.
\ese
\ed

\bt
Let $f \cl \R \to \R$ be a smooth map. Then
\bi{rCl}
f(x + \ve) &=& f(x) + \ve f'(x) \\
&& {} + \cO(\ve^2).
\ei
\et

\br
The notation $\cO(\ve^2)$ denotes terms of order $\ve^2$ and higher.
\er

\index{topological space}
\label{sec:topology}

This uses \bm{v} for bold and $\Z$ for integers.
